steps.title: Identify where the y-intercept occurs on the coordinate plane
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis. On the y-axis, the x-coordinate is always 0. Therefore, the y-intercept is at the point $(0, k)$, where $k$ is the y-coordinate we're told about.

steps.title: Apply function notation at x = 0
steps.content: By definition, $h(0)$ is the output of the function when $x = 0$. Since the y-intercept is the point $(0, k)$, we have $h(0) = k$.

steps.title: Check all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $h(0)$: The y-intercept always occurs at $x = 0$, so $h(0)$ gives the y-coordinate $k$ ✓
  • Choice B — $h(2)$: $x = 2$ is not the y-axis; $h(2)$ gives the output at $x = 2$, not the y-intercept ✗
  • Choice C — $h(4)$: $x = 4$ is not the y-axis; same reasoning as B ✗
  • Choice D — $h(6)$: $x = 6$ is not the y-axis; same reasoning as B ✗
That is the correct answer.
